fault_hypothesis: Line 70 truncates planned allocations with `int(x)` (floor) instead of applying largest-remainder rounding, so the sum of allocations can be less than `total` when fractional remainders are discarded.

constraints.intent: After building allocations by int-truncation, their sum must equal the rounded sum of planned values (i.e., total); this is violated when remainders are dropped.

spec.expr: sum(allocations__1) == sum(int(x) for x in planned__1) and sum(allocations__1) == round(sum(planned__1))

constraints.intent: Immediately after allocations is defined, its sum must match the rounded total of planned values, catching the floor-truncation loss.

spec.expr: abs(sum(planned__1) - round(sum(planned__1))) < 1e-9 or sum(int(x) for x in planned__1) == round(sum(planned__1))

constraints.intent: Before planned is consumed by the list comprehension, verify that simple floor-truncation would preserve the total; if not, the comprehension on line 70 is wrong.
The returned allocations must sum to the original total; failing tests show this is violated.
